{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01Q4oagYCev4JsKggsXNfi2u","type":"tool_result","content":"/app/.claude-harness/hooks/tbench_hooks.py\n#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nTerminal-Bench Claude Code Hooks (unified script).\n\nHandles all three hook events via stdin payload inspection:\n- PreToolUse: tracks state, always allows\n- PostToolUse: tracks errors, verification results, loop detection\n- Stop: blocks exit if verification hasn't been attempted\n\nProtocol:\n  Input: JSON via stdin\n  Output: JSON via stdout wrapped in {\"hookSpecificOutput\": {...}}\n\nState persisted in /tmp/.tbench_state.json across invocations.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nSTATE_FILE = Path(\"/tmp/.tbench_state.json\")\n\n# Patterns that indicate the agent is running verification tests\nVERIFICATION_PATTERNS = [\n    \"tests/test.sh\",\n    \"tests/test_\",\n    \"pytest tests/\",\n    \"pytest -v\",\n    \"python -m pytest\",\n    \"python3 -m pytest\",\n    \"make test\",\n    \"cargo test\",\n    \"npm test\",\n    \"go test\",\n    \"bash test\",\n    \"./test.sh\",\n    \"ctest\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate test success\nSUCCESS_PATTERNS = [\n    \"passed\",\n    \" ok\",\n    \"0 failed\",\n    \"tests passed\",\n    \"all tests\",\n    \"PASS\",\n    \"OK (\",\n    \"0 errors\",\n    \"Build succeeded\",\n    \"All checks passed\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate errors in command output\nERROR_PATTERNS = [\n    \"error:\",\n    \"Error:\",\n    \"ERROR:\",\n    \"traceback\",\n    \"Traceback\",\n    \"FAILED\",\n    \"not found\",\n    \"No such file\",\n    \"permission denied\",\n    \"Permission denied\",\n    \"command not found\",\n    \"ModuleNotFoundError\",\n    \"ImportError\",\n    \"SyntaxError\",\n    \"NameError\",\n    \"TypeError\",\n    \"ValueError\",\n    \"FileNotFoundError\",\n    \"ConnectionRefusedError\",\n    \"OSError\",\n    \"RuntimeError\",\n    \"segmentation fault\",\n    \"Segmentation fault\",\n    \"core dumped\",\n    \"killed\",\n    \"Killed\",\n    \"Critical error:\",\n    \"AssertionError\",\n]\n\n# Common build artifacts that should be cleaned up\nBUILD_ARTIFACT_PATTERNS = [\n    \"a.out\",\n    \"*.o\",\n    \"*.obj\",\n    \"*.exe\",\n    \"*.dll\",\n    \"*.so\",\n    \"*.dylib\",\n    \"*.pyc\",\n    \"__pycache__\",\n    \".DS_Store\",\n    \"*.swp\",\n    \"*.swo\",\n    \"core\",\n    \"*.core\",\n]\n\n# Deliverable patterns - files that should NOT be cleaned\nDELIVERABLE_PATTERNS = [\n    \"main.py.c\",\n    \"main.c.py\",\n    \"move.txt\",\n    \"regex.txt\",\n    \"response.txt\",\n    \"solution.py\",\n    \"answer.txt\",\n    \"output.json\",\n    \"result.csv\",\n]\n\n\ndef load_state() -> dict:\n    if STATE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            return json.loads(STATE_FILE.read_text())\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n            pass\n    return {\n        \"start_time\": time.time(),\n        \"turn_count\": 0,\n        \"file_edits\": {},\n        \"verification_attempted\": False,\n        \"verification_passed\": False,\n        \"consecutive_failures\": 0,\n        \"last_error_sig\": \"\",\n        \"stop_block_count\": 0,\n    }\n\n\ndef save_state(state: dict) -> None:\n    try:\n        STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state))\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n\n\ndef emit(payload: dict) -> None:\n    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout)\n\n\ndef emit_allow(reason: str = \"\") -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\",\n            \"permissionDecision\": \"allow\",\n            \"permissionDecisionReason\": reason,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef emit_notification(event: str, message: str) -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": event,\n            \"notification\": message,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef is_verification_command(cmd: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a command is running verification tests.\"\"\"\n    return any(pattern in cmd for pattern in VERIFICATION_PATTERNS)\n\n\ndef detect_hook_type(data: dict) -> str:\n    if \"tool_output\" in data:\n        return \"PostToolUse\"\n    if \"tool_name\" in data:\n        return \"PreToolUse\"\n    return \"Stop\"\n\n\ndef handle_pretool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n    state[\"turn_count\"] = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0) + 1\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_input = data.get(\"tool_input\", {})\n\n    # Track file edits for loop detection\n    if tool_name in (\"Edit\", \"Write\"):\n        filepath = tool_input.get(\"file_path\", \"unknown\")\n        state[\"file_edits\"][filepath] = state[\"file_edits\"].get(filepath, 0) + 1\n\n    # Track verification attempts\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        cmd = tool_input.get(\"command\", \"\")\n        if is_verification_command(cmd):\n            if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n                # Reset stop counter when entering verification phase\n                state[\"stop_block_count\"] = 0\n            state[\"verification_attempted\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n    emit_allow(\"[tbench]\")\n\n\ndef handle_posttool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_output = str(data.get(\"tool_output\", \"\"))\n\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        # Detect errors\n        has_error = any(err in tool_output for err in ERROR_PATTERNS)\n\n        if has_error:\n            sig = tool_output[:200]\n            if sig == state.get(\"last_error_sig\", \"\"):\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) + 1\n            else:\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 1\n                state[\"last_error_sig\"] = sig\n        else:\n            state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 0\n\n        # Detect verification success\n        if state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n            if any(kw in tool_output for kw in SUCCESS_PATTERNS):\n                state[\"verification_passed\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n\n    # Emit warnings for loops\n    notifications = []\n\n    for filepath, count in state.get(\"file_edits\", {}).items():\n        if count >= 4:\n            notifications.append(\n                f\"[tbench] WARNING: {filepath} edited {count} times. \"\n                \"STOP editing this file. Re-read the requirements and try \"\n                \"a fundamentally different approach.\"\n            )\n\n    if state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) >= 3:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: {state['consecutive_failures']} consecutive failures \"\n            \"with the same error. Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n        )\n\n    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n            \"STOP what you're doing. Run the tests NOW. Fix only what fails.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 40 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Run verification NOW to check progress. \"\n            \"Early feedback prevents wasted turns.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 150:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. You are running low on turns. \"\n            \"Wrap up — ensure tests pass and stop.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 120 and not state.get(\"verification_passed\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Tests not yet passing. \"\n            \"Simplify your approach. A minimal passing solution beats a failed ambitious one.\"\n        )\n\n    if notifications:\n        emit_notification(\"PostToolUse\", \" | \".join(notifications))\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef handle_stop(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        # After 3 blocks, let it go (avoid infinite loop)\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 3:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] BLOCKED: You have NOT run the test suite. \"\n            \"You MUST verify before stopping. Run: \"\n            \"cd /app && bash tests/test.sh \"\n            \"OR: cd /app && python3 -m pytest tests/ -v\"\n        )\n    elif not state.get(\"verification_passed\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 2:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] Tests were run but appear to have failed. \"\n            \"Review the test output, fix issues, and re-run verification. \"\n            \"If you believe tests actually passed, you may stop.\"\n        )\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    try:\n        data = json.load(sys.stdin)\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, EOFError):\n        data = {}\n\n    hook_type = detect_hook_type(data)\n\n    if hook_type == \"PreToolUse\":\n        handle_pretool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"PostToolUse\":\n        handle_posttool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"Stop\":\n        handle_stop(data)\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"abb87611-bf32-4f31-b5ec-8b4e0a003034","uuid":"89b093f4-d10c-48a3-836b-a6ee34570580","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"/app/.claude-harness/hooks/tbench_hooks.py\n#!/usr/bin/env {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01HcUaPJfBuH7kgUDH5DqSBu","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_017T9Z6Uary3Zu5cTrVuWn3k","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'PYEOF'\n# Analyze the sequences and find junctions\ndef parse_fasta(filepath):\n    seqs = {}\n    name = None\n    seq = []\n    with open(filepath) as f:\n        for line in f:\n            line = line.strip()\n            if line.startswith('>'):\n                if name:\n                    seqs[name] = ''.join(seq)\n                name = line[1:]\n                seq = []\n            else:\n                seq.append(line)\n    if name:\n        seqs[name] = ''.join(seq)\n    return seqs\n\nseqs = parse_fasta('/app/sequences.fasta')\nfor name in seqs:\n    print(f\"{name}: {len(seqs[name])} bp\")\n\ninp = seqs['input']\nout = seqs['output']\negfp = seqs['egfp']\nflag = seqs['flag']\nsnap = seqs['snap']\n\n# Find where the inserts are in the output\n# EGFP without ATG and TAA\negfp_body = egfp[3:-3]  # skip ATG and stop codon\nflag_body = flag[3:-3]   # skip ATG and stop codon  \nsnap_body = snap[3:-3]   # skip ATG and stop codon (taa for snap is actually the last 3 chars)\n\nprint(f\"\\negfp starts with: {egfp[:10]}, ends with: {egfp[-10:]}\")\nprint(f\"flag starts with: {flag[:10]}, ends with: {flag[-10:]}\")\nprint(f\"snap starts with: {snap[:10]}, ends with: {snap[-10:]}\")\n\n# Check what snap's stop codon is\nprint(f\"\\nsnap last 6: {snap[-6:]}\")  # Check if it ends with taa, tag, or tga\nprint(f\"flag last 6: {flag[-6:]}\")\nprint(f\"egfp last 6: {egfp[-6:]}\")\n\n# Find egfp in output (without ATG)\negfp_noATG = egfp[3:]  # everything after ATG\npos = out.find(egfp_noATG)\nprint(f\"\\negfp (no ATG) found in output at position: {pos}\")\n\n# Find the full inserts in the output\n# The output has egfp+flag+snap fused together\n# Let's find the exact positions\negfp_noATG_noStop = egfp[3:-3]\nflag_noATG_noStop = flag[3:-3]\nsnap_noATG = snap[3:]\n\nprint(f\"\\negfp (no ATG, no stop): {egfp_noATG_noStop[:20]}...{egfp_noATG_noStop[-20:]}\")\nprint(f\"flag (no ATG, no stop): {flag_noATG_noStop[:20]}...{flag_noATG_noStop[-20:]}\")\nprint(f\"snap (no ATG): {snap_noATG[:20]}...{snap_noATG[-20:]}\")\n\n# Find positions in output\npos_egfp = out.find(egfp_noATG_noStop)\npos_flag = out.find(flag_noATG_noStop)\npos_snap = out.find(snap_noATG)\n\nprint(f\"\\nPositions in output:\")\nprint(f\"  egfp body at: {pos_egfp} to {pos_egfp + len(egfp_noATG_noStop) - 1}\")\nprint(f\"  flag body at: {pos_flag} to {pos_flag + len(flag_noATG_noStop) - 1}\")\nprint(f\"  snap body at: {pos_snap} to {pos_snap + len(snap_noATG) - 1}\")\n\n# Show the junction regions\nprint(f\"\\nJunction 1 (Vector→EGFP): output[{pos_egfp-10}:{pos_egfp+10}] = '{out[pos_egfp-10:pos_egfp+10]}'\")\nprint(f\"Junction 2 (EGFP→FLAG):   output[{pos_flag-10}:{pos_flag+10}] = '{out[pos_flag-10:pos_flag+10]}'\")\nprint(f\"Junction 3 (FLAG→SNAP):   output[{pos_snap-10}:{pos_snap+10}] = '{out[pos_snap-10:pos_snap+10]}'\")\n\nsnap_end = pos_snap + len(snap_noATG)\nprint(f\"Junction 4 (SNAP→Vector): output[{snap_end-10}:{snap_end+10}] = '{out[snap_end-10:snap_end+10]}'\")\n\n# Now find the vector parts\n# Vector before insert: ends right before egfp body starts\n# Since output starts with the vector and egfp_noATG_noStop starts at pos_egfp,\n# but the ATG is shared with the vector context\nprint(f\"\\nOutput around start of egfp:\")\nprint(f\"  output[{pos_egfp-20}:{pos_egfp+20}] = '{out[pos_egfp-20:pos_egfp+20]}'\")\n\n# Check: does the ATG before egfp body exist in the input?\n# In input: ...atatacatatgatcagtctg...\n# In output: ...atatacatatgagcaagggc... (where agcaagggc is start of egfp body)\n# So position pos_egfp - 3 should be the ATG\nprint(f\"\\n3 chars before egfp body: '{out[pos_egfp-3:pos_egfp]}'\")\nprint(f\"Should be 'atg' (shared start codon)\")\n\n# After snap ends\nprint(f\"\\nOutput after snap: '{out[snap_end:snap_end+20]}'\")\nprint(f\"Input has similar: \", inp.find(out[snap_end:snap_end+20]))\n\nPYEOF","description":"Analyze sequences to find junction points"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":279,"cache_read_input_tokens":51972,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":279,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"abb87611-bf32-4f31-b5ec-8b4e0a003034","uuid":"d5df35b4-b737-4faa-95df-7c51a97b415d"}
